summary_one_line: Structured glyph-record bytes that drive an Indic Ra+Halant syllable to form a Reph (POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH), reached by plain havoc not I2S.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input whose decoded glyph stream forms an Indic consonant syllable beginning with Ra+Halant in a Reph-capable script, so that has_reph becomes true and info[start] is tagged POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH, making the loop at line 849 take its true side. Winner seeds carry repeated 4-byte little-endian glyph-record words (e.g. bf 0c 00 00) rather than ASCII sfnt table tags.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The naive winner-resolving seed is filled with structured repeated 4-byte records (bf 0c 00 00 ... bf 0c 00 00) that decode into the glyph sequence needed to set POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH; the W role hits line 850 (the reph-mask loop body) and line 644 (info[start].indic_position() = POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH), both W-only.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser-blocking seeds are dominated by ASCII sfnt table tags surfaced by CMP interception (kern at offset 0x05, GPOS spelled by G/P/O/S at offsets 0x0c-0x0f); these steer the corpus toward valid-table-name inputs on non-reph paths, so info[start].indic_position() never equals POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH and line 849 stays on its false side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, which has no input-to-state mutator and reaches the branch by plain havoc byte mutation (BytesCopyMutator/ByteAddMutator/BytesSetMutator) drifting into the structured-glyph-record byte regime. The delta is the comparison-solving technique: cmplog substitutes intercepted font-table-tag operands (kern/GPOS) back into the input, fixating the cmplog corpus on table-name strings and steering it AWAY from the glyph-record bytes needed for Reph. This is an anti-comparison-solving effect: the enabling technique for the winner is havoc-only byte mutation, and the comparison-solving substitution actively hurts the loser.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive>cmplog with delta I2S; naive is the winner and cmplog the loser.
The blocked true side is the Reph loop gated on indic_position() == POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: for (unsigned int i = start; i < end && info[i].indic_position() == POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH; i++)

evidence_trail.claim: POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH is assigned only when has_reph is set, i.e. when a Ra-initial reph-forming syllable was detected.
The naive winner-resolving seed is built from repeated 4-byte structured glyph-record words rather than ASCII text.
The W role hits the reph-mask loop body at line 850 that the L role does not.
The cmplog loser-blocking seeds contain the ASCII sfnt table tag 'kern'.
Across the loser seeds the bytes at offsets 0x0c-0x0f concentrate on G/P/O/S, spelling the sfnt table tag GPOS.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes intercepted CMP operands (such as table-name strings) back into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, so the winner reaches the branch by plain havoc byte mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5857 --role W --fuzzer naive --trial 2 --seed 61118ed1d1d7f65e`. The winner is naive (no I2S mutator), so this is an anti-I2S divergence and I2SRandReplace is NOT the winning mechanism; lineage verification of an I2S floor is not applicable. The chain is havoc/token mutators end to end (BytesCopyMutator, ByteAddMutator, ByteInterestingMutator, etc.). It does report 1 dash row at depth 24, but the prompt's caveat states dash-row=I2S holds only for cmplog/vpc; naive uses plain LineageMutator and cannot produce an I2S find, so that lone deep dash row is a cross-pollinated/early-corpus ancestor, not evidence of I2S in the winner.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where seeding the cmplog CMP buffer with the Indic glyph-record byte values needed for POS_RA_TO_BECOME_REPH lets cmplog resolve the branch as fast as or faster than naive — showing I2S does not steer the corpus away from the reph-forming bytes.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that I2S actively diverts cmplog toward font-table-tag strings (kern/GPOS) is inferred from loser-seed byte content plus the I2SRandReplace description, not from a direct trace of which CMP operands cmplog substituted.
